children.heading: .—Authority of secretary of treasury over certain audits and investigations
children.label: 1 .—Authority of secretary of treasury over certain audits and investigations
A
children.text: ongoing criminal investigations or proceedings;

children.label: B
children.text: undercover operations;

children.label: C
children.text: the identity of confidential sources, including protected witnesses;

children.label: D
children.text: deliberations and decisions on policy matters, including documented information used as a basis for making policy decisions, the disclosure of which could reasonably be expected to have a significant influence on the economy or market behavior;

children.label: E
children.text: intelligence or counterintelligence matters; or

children.label: F
children.text: other matters the disclosure of which would constitute a serious threat to national security or to the protection of any person or property authorized protection by , , or any provision of the Presidential Protection Assistance Act of 1976 (, note).section 3056 of title 18section 3056A of title 18Public Law 94–52418 U.S.C. 3056

children.heading: .—Authority of secretary of treasury to prohibit carrying out or completing certain audits and investigations
children.label: 2 .—Authority of secretary of treasury to prohibit carrying out or completing certain audits and investigations
children.text: With respect to the information described under paragraph (1), the Secretary of the Treasury may prohibit the Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury from carrying out or completing any audit or investigation, from accessing information described in paragraph (1), or from issuing any subpoena, after such Inspector General has decided to initiate, carry out, or complete such audit or investigation, access such information, or to issue such subpoena, if the Secretary determines that such prohibition is necessary to prevent the disclosure of any information described under paragraph (1) or to prevent significant impairment to the national interests of the United States.

children.heading: .—Notification and statement of reasons for exercise of power
children.label: 3 .—Notification and statement of reasons for exercise of power
children.text: If the Secretary of the Treasury exercises any power under paragraph (1) or (2), the Secretary of the Treasury shall notify the Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury in writing, stating the reasons for such exercise. Within 30 days after receipt of any such notice, the Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury shall transmit a copy of such notice to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs and the Committee on Finance of the Senate and the Committee on Oversight and Reform and the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives, and to other appropriate committees or subcommittees of the Congress.

children.heading: .—Exception relating to treasury inspector general for tax administration
children.label: 4 .—Exception relating to treasury inspector general for tax administration
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury may not exercise any power under paragraph (1) or (2) with respect to the Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration.

children.heading: Oversight Responsibility for Internal Investigations.—
children.label: b Oversight Responsibility for Internal Investigations.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: In carrying out the duties and responsibilities specified in this chapter, the Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury shall have oversight responsibility for the internal investigations performed by the Office of Internal Affairs of the Tax and Trade Bureau. The head of such office shall promptly report to the Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury the significant activities being carried out by such office.

children.heading: .—Exercise of duties and responsibilities
children.label: 2 .—Exercise of duties and responsibilities
children.text: The Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury shall exercise all duties and responsibilities of an Inspector General for the Department of the Treasury other than the duties and responsibilities exercised by the Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration.

children.label: A
children.text: determine how audits and investigations are allocated in cases of overlapping jurisdiction; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide for coordination, cooperation, and efficiency in the conduct of such audits and investigations.

children.heading: .—Audits and Investigations in Department of Treasury
children.label: c .—Audits and Investigations in Department of Treasury
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (b), the Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury may initiate, conduct and supervise such audits and investigations in the Department of the Treasury (including the bureau referred to in subsection (b)) as the Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury considers appropriate.

children.heading: .—Authority To Provide Written Notice to Tax and Trade Bureau
children.label: d .—Authority To Provide Written Notice to Tax and Trade Bureau
children.text: If the Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury initiates an audit or investigation under subsection (c) concerning the bureau referred to in subsection (b), the Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury may provide the head of the office of such bureau referred to in subsection (b) with written notice that the Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury has initiated such an audit or investigation. If the Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury issues a notice under the preceding sentence, no other audit or investigation shall be initiated into the matter under audit or investigation by the Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury, and any other audit or investigation of such matter shall cease.

children.heading: Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration.—
children.label: e Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration.—

children.heading: .—Access to returns and return information
children.label: 1 .—Access to returns and return information
children.text: The Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration shall have access to returns and return information, as defined in section 6103(b) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (), only in accordance with the provisions of section 6103 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 () and this chapter.26 U.S.C. 6103(b)26 U.S.C. 6103

children.heading: .—Standardized records and accountings
children.label: 2 .—Standardized records and accountings
children.text: The Internal Revenue Service shall maintain the same system of standardized records or accountings of all requests from the Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration for inspection or disclosure of returns and return information (including the reasons for and dates of such requests), and of returns and return information inspected or disclosed pursuant to such requests, as described under section 6103(p)(3)(A) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (). Such system of standardized records or accountings shall also be available for examination in the same manner as provided under section 6103(p)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 ().26 U.S.C. 6103(p)(3)(A)26 U.S.C. 6103(p)(3)

children.heading: .—Safeguards and conditions
children.label: 3 .—Safeguards and conditions
children.text: The Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration shall be subject to the same safeguards and conditions for receiving returns and return information as are described under section 6103(p)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 ().26 U.S.C. 6103(p)(4)

children.heading: 6406 1986.—Audit or Investigation Shall Not Affect Final Decision Under Sectionof Internal Revenue Code of
children.label: f 6406 1986.—Audit or Investigation Shall Not Affect Final Decision Under Sectionof Internal Revenue Code of
children.text: An audit or investigation conducted by the Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury or the Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration shall not affect a final decision of the Secretary of the Treasury or the Secretary’s delegate under section 6406 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 ().26 U.S.C. 6406

children.heading: .—Reports to congressional committees
children.label: 1 .—Reports to congressional committees
children.text: Any report required to be transmitted by the Secretary of the Treasury to the appropriate committees or subcommittees of the Congress under shall also be transmitted, within the 7-day period specified under such section, to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs and the Committee on Finance of the Senate and the Committee on Oversight and Reform and the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives.section 405(e) of this title

children.heading: .—Reports made by treasury inspector general for tax administration to congressional committees
children.label: 2 .—Reports made by treasury inspector general for tax administration to congressional committees
children.text: Any report made by the Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration that is required to be transmitted by the Secretary of the Treasury to the appropriate committees or subcommittees of Congress under shall also be transmitted, within the 7-day period specified under such subsection, to the Internal Revenue Service Oversight Board and the Commissioner of Internal Revenue.section 405(e) of this title

children.heading: .—Duties and Responsibilities of Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration
children.label: h .—Duties and Responsibilities of Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration
children.text: The Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration shall exercise all duties and responsibilities of an Inspector General of an establishment with respect to the Department of the Treasury and the Secretary of the Treasury on all matters relating to the Internal Revenue Service. The Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration shall have sole authority under this chapter to conduct an audit or investigation of the Internal Revenue Service Oversight Board and the Chief Counsel for the Internal Revenue Service.

children.heading: .—Ability To Lead Large and Complex Organization
children.label: i .—Ability To Lead Large and Complex Organization
children.text: In addition to the requirements of the 1st sentence of , the Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration should have demonstrated ability to lead a large and complex organization.section 403(a) of this title

children.heading: .—Prohibition on Appointment of Employee of Internal Revenue Service to Certain Positions
children.label: j .—Prohibition on Appointment of Employee of Internal Revenue Service to Certain Positions

children.text: during the 2-year period preceding the date of appointment to such position; or
during the 5-year period following the date such individual ends service in such position.

children.label: A
children.text: shall have the duty to enforce criminal provisions under section 7608(b) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 ();26 U.S.C. 7608(b)

children.label: B
children.text: in addition to the functions authorized under section 7608(b)(2) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (), may carry firearms;26 U.S.C. 7608(b)(2)

children.label: C
children.text: shall be responsible for protecting the Internal Revenue Service against external attempts to corrupt or threaten employees of the Internal Revenue Service, but shall not be responsible for the conducting of background checks and the providing of protection to the Commissioner of Internal Revenue; and

children.label: D
children.text: may designate any employee in the Office of the Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration to enforce such laws and perform such functions referred to under subparagraphs (A), (B), and (C).

children.heading: .—Reporting reasonable grounds to believe a violation of federal criminal law occurred
children.label: A .—Reporting reasonable grounds to believe a violation of federal criminal law occurred
children.text: In performing a law enforcement function under paragraph (1), the Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration shall report any reasonable grounds to believe there has been a violation of Federal criminal law to the Attorney General at an appropriate time as determined by the Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration, notwithstanding .section 404(d) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the performance of a law enforcement function under paragraph (1); and

children.label: ii
children.text: sensitive information concerning matters under subsection (a)(1)(A) through (F).

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: 3 .—Limitation
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to affect the authority of any other person to carry out or enforce any provision specified in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Request for Audit or Investigation Relating to Internal Revenue Service.—
children.label: l Request for Audit or Investigation Relating to Internal Revenue Service.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Commissioner of Internal Revenue or the Internal Revenue Service Oversight Board may request, in writing, the Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration to conduct an audit or investigation relating to the Internal Revenue Service. If the Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration determines not to conduct such audit or investigation, the Inspector General shall timely provide a written explanation for such determination to the person making the request.

children.heading: .—Final report of audit
children.label: A .—Final report of audit
children.text: Any final report of an audit conducted by the Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration shall be timely submitted by the Inspector General to the Commissioner of Internal Revenue and the Internal Revenue Service Oversight Board.

children.heading: .—Periodic list of investigations for which final report completed
children.label: B .—Periodic list of investigations for which final report completed
children.text: The Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration shall periodically submit to the Commissioner and Board a list of investigations for which a final report has been completed by the Inspector General and shall provide a copy of any such report upon request of the Commissioner or Board.

children.heading: .—Applicability
children.label: C .—Applicability
children.text: This paragraph applies regardless of whether the applicable audit or investigation is requested under paragraph (1).
